commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=92f7941c7654794a61e70679f0d3784051205279)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Bailian to vLLM/Qwen thinking-compat checks in configured-model resolution, changes the vLLM thinking-policy provider gate, and adds Bailian-focused vLLM policy tests.

PR surface: Source 0, Tests +83. Total +83 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level. Current main keeps the relevant configured-model inference vLLM-only, and the PR's new `openai` thinkingFormat expectation does not match the current vLLM resolver.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review is needed because the branch lacks real behavior proof and the remaining blocker is provider ownership/model-support scope rather than a narrow automated repair.

target.title: fix: allow bailian provider in resolveThinkingProfile
target.body:
## Problem

The thinking profile resolver and Qwen thinking compatibility checks
hardcode `provider === "vllm"`, excluding `bailian` (Alibaba Cloud)
even when `compat.thinkingFormat` is correctly configured.

This prevents Bailian-hosted Qwen/GLM models from being recognized
as reasoning-capable, breaking `/thinking` commands and reasoning
token tracking.

## Root Cause

Three locations check for `vllm` provider without considering that
other providers (like `bailian`) may also serve Qwen-format models:

1. `extensions/vllm/thinking-policy.ts` — resolveThinkingProfile()
2. `src/agents/model-selection-shared.ts` — isVllmQwenThinkingCompat()
3. `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/model.ts` — readCompatThinkingFormat()

## Fix

Extend each check to accept both `vllm` and `bailian` providers.

## Verified Configurations

Tested with Bailian provider using both thinking formats:

| Model | thinkingFormat | Status |
|-------|---------------|--------|
| qwen3.7-plus | openai | ✅ working |
| qwen3.6-plus | qwen | ✅ working |
| qwen3-max-2026-01-23 | openai | ✅ working |
| glm-5 | openai | ✅ working |

## Workaround (before this fix)

Users must manually patch `/app/dist/*.js` after each upgrade and
set `reasoning: true` in config for each model.

Closes #26037
target.author: andreacasini
